title: hw3_repo: specification package structure
content:
## HW3 Specification Package — Virtual Card Lifecycle

### Deliverables (homework-3/)
- `specification.md` — 1,903 lines, 19 sections (v1.0-FINAL)
- `agents.md` — 3-agent workflow, 247 lines
- `rules/.cursorrules` — 28 AI/editor rules, 199 lines
- `README.md` — rationale + 10 best practices, 170 lines

### Supporting artefacts
- `hw3-planning-architecture.md` — Phase 1 scope evaluation & agent design
- `hw3-agent1-phase1-foundation.md` — Agent 1 Pass 1 output
- `hw3-agent2-compliance-overlay.md` — Agent 2 Pass 1 (compliance/risk)
- `TASKS.md` — original homework brief

### Specification structure (19 sections)
1. Document Control
2. Vision and Product Context (incl. Data Classification Matrix)
3. Stakeholders and Personas (6 roles: CARDHOLDER, PROGRAM_MANAGER, FRAUD_OPS, OPERATIONS_READ, COMPLIANCE_READ, SUPPORT)
4. Scope and Out-of-Scope (5 in-scope subsystems, 11 excluded)
5. High-Level Objective (HLO-01)
6. Mid-Level Objectives (OBJ-01–07 with measurable success criteria)
7. Regulatory, Security, and Compliance (COMP-01–12, SEC-01–12, AUD-01–29, AUD-RET-01–06)
8. Non-Functional Requirements (NFR-01–21)
9. Subsystem Specifications (SYS-01–05 with REQs, permissions, edge cases, verification)
10. State Machines (4 Mermaid diagrams: card lifecycle, freeze flow, transaction FSM, limit evaluation)
11. API Contracts (15 endpoints with schemas, error codes, SCA/idempotency requirements)
12. Data Models (7 models: VirtualCard, SpendingLimit, TransactionRecord, AuditEvent, WebhookRegistration, WebhookDeliveryAttempt, RiskSignal)
13. Edge Cases and Failure Modes (22 EDGE + 10 FAIL)
14. Low-Level Task Decomposition (34 tasks in 7 groups)
15. Beginning/Ending Context
16. Verification Strategy (6 categories)
17. Traceability Matrix
18. Glossary (24 terms)
19. Open Questions (8, all with assumptions documented)
